operating_state: Nike revenues declined 10% to $46.3 billion driven by broad-based weakness across all geographic segments with North America, EMEA, and Greater China experiencing high single to double-digit declines. NIKE Direct revenues decreased 13% with digital sales declining 20% while wholesale revenues fell 6%, reflecting reduced consumer demand and decreased traffic across channels. Footwear revenues dropped 11% with 8% unit decline and 3 percentage point ASP reduction from higher discounts and unfavorable channel mix shifts toward promotional activity.
cost_structure: Gross margin contracted 280 basis points to 43.6% primarily from lower average selling prices due to increased promotional activity and channel mix shifts, partially offset by reduced product costs and higher inventory obsolescence reserves. Operating expenses increased 1% with demand creation expense rising from higher brand marketing investments while operating overhead decreased from lower wage-related costs and prior year restructuring benefits. Manufacturing costs face ongoing pressure from commodity price volatility and supply chain disruptions affecting raw material availability and pricing.
material_events: Company returned $5.3 billion to shareholders through share repurchases under the four-year $18 billion program and dividends, while repaying $1.0 billion in bond obligations. Eliminated President, Consumer, Product & Brand role with Heidi O'Neill transitioning out effective May 2025 and nominated Jørgen Vig Knudstorp for director election. Standard & Poor's downgraded long-term debt rating from AA- to A+ while maintaining Investment Grade status.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation negatively impacted revenues by $419 million and income before taxes by $97 million due to stronger U.S. Dollar, with Greater China gross margin contracting 420 basis points from unfavorable exchange rates. New tariffs expected to materially increase cost of sales with negative gross margin impact anticipated for fiscal 2026 despite mitigation efforts. OECD global minimum tax rate of 15% became effective with several countries implementing domestic legislation affecting tax obligations.
forward_outlook: Management repositioning NIKE Brand Digital as full-price platform while reinvesting in wholesale distribution channels, focusing on driving distinction within key sports and building complete product portfolio. Strategy includes continued inventory liquidation through increased markdowns and higher wholesale returns to rebalance portfolio mix. Company filed new shelf registration statement and entered new committed credit facilities while moderating share repurchases based on liquidity and operating cash flows.
uncertainty_profile: European Commission investigation into Netherlands tax rulings could result in additional tax assessments with estimated future income tax obligations related to expected IRS resolution. Outstanding endorsement contract obligations with payments due within 12 months, though actual payments may vary based on athletic performance. Evolving regulations around generative AI, data privacy, and supply chain reporting requirements may increase operating costs and compliance obligations.
